channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: GLP-1 Medication Semaglutide Shows Association with Reduced Depression and Anxiety
item.description: A large-scale study conducted by the University of Eastern Finland, Karolinska Institutet in Sweden, and Griffith University in Australia has found that GLP-1 receptor agonists, including semaglutide ...

item.title: Free State individuals arrested for impersonating mayor and misusing blue lights
item.description: In Sandton, Gauteng, two individuals were apprehended for impersonating a mayor and a bodyguard from the Free State. The Johannesburg Metropolitan Police Department (JMPD) stopped a Haval vehicle fitt...

item.title: Impact of Electoral Violence on Businesses and the Economy in Kenya
item.description: Kenya has a long history of electoral violence, and this continues to disrupt businesses across the country. From the 2007/08 post-election crisis to recent protests, political instability has scared ...

item.title: Boeing Goes Court Over 737 MAX Crash Affecting American NGO Worker
item.description: Boeing don dey prepare to waka enter court again Monday as e go face another lawsuit wey concern the 737 MAX crash wey happen for Ethiopia back in March 2019. The case na from the family of Samya Stum...

item.title: Deployment of AI Cameras in Western US Helps Detect Wildfires Early
item.description: States across the fire-prone Western United States are increasingly deploying artificial intelligence (AI) cameras to detect wildfires at an early stage. In Arizona, an AI camera in Coconino National ...

item.title: SANEF initiates independent inquiry into allegations against Sunday Times editor
item.description: The South African National Editors’ Forum (SANEF) has initiated an independent legal inquiry following the Special Investigating Unit's (SIU) report implicating Sunday Times editor Makhudu Sefara in a...

item.title: SAWS Issues Warning for Cold, Wet and Windy Weather Across South Africa
item.description: South Africans are being advised to prepare for a spell of severe weather as the South African Weather Service (SAWS) forecasts a powerful cut-off low-pressure system moving across the country from Mo...

item.title: Tanzanian Researcher Wins Harvard Award for AI-Based Youth Loan Solutions
item.description: Ngasuma Kanyeka, a Tanzanian PhD candidate, has been honoured by the Harvard Ministerial Leadership Programme for her groundbreaking work in using Artificial Intelligence to improve credit systems. He...

item.title: LTFRB to issue show cause orders to 21 transport network companies for unauthorized vehicles
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) announced that it will issue show cause orders to 21 transport network companies (TNCs) for operating unauthorized units. This action a...

item.title: 5.2 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Islamabad and Peshawar, No Casualties Reported
item.description: On May 4, 2026, a 5.2-magnitude earthquake struck Islamabad, Peshawar, and several regions in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, according to the Seismic Monitoring Centre. The tremor, which was recorded at a depth ...

item.title: Mercy Oketch Optimistic After Fourth Place Finish at World Relays
item.description: Kenya's sprinter Mercy Oketch remains positive despite narrowly missing out on a medal at the World Relays in Botswana. The national record holder, part of the 4x400m mixed relay team, helped the quar...

item.title: Ewseta Under Fire for R58 Million Waste on Unused Johannesburg Property
item.description: The Energy and Water Sector Education and Training Authority (Ewseta) has come under serious scrutiny after more than R58 million in public funds was spent on a Johannesburg property that has never be...

item.title: Overview of the US Military Deployment in Europe Amid Planned Troop Reductions
item.description: The United States currently maintains a significant military presence in Europe, with approximately 80,000 to 100,000 troops stationed across the continent, including over 36,000 in Germany. This depl...

item.title: 12 People Injured in Shooting at Oklahoma Party
item.description: A shooting incident at a party in Oklahoma resulted in at least 12 people being wounded. Local authorities responded promptly to the scene, providing medical attention to the victims and securing the ...

item.title: Russia's Hybrid Strategy in Africa: Combining Africa Corps Military Presence with Soft Power Tools
item.description: Russia is making a big comeback in Africa after many years of less activity following the end of the Soviet Union. This move has become faster because of Moscow's isolation from Western countries sinc...

item.title: Police register abetment case following death of 30-year-old Delhi judge
item.description: In a tragic incident that has shocked the legal community, a 30-year-old judicial officer was found dead at his residence in south Delhi’s Green Park area on May 2, 2026. The deceased, identified as A...

item.description: This article provides daily horoscope insights for May 4, 2026, offering guidance based on astrological influences. For those celebrating a birthday today, a Mars-Jupiter alignment enhances self-confi...

item.title: Exploring Lee Ufan’s Art and Philosophy on Japan’s Naoshima Island
item.description: Naoshima, once a heavily industrialised island in Japan, has transformed into a haven for contemplative art, largely through the vision of billionaire Sōichirō Fukutake and the work of Korea-born arti...

item.title: IHC to Hear Main Appeals of Imran Khan and Bushra Bibi in £190 Million Corruption Case on May 7
item.description: On 4th May 2026, Islamabad High Court (IHC) turned down the applications of former Prime Minister Imran Khan and his spouse Bushra Bibi seeking suspension of their prison sentences in the £190 million...

item.title: Top 5 Shows and Films to Stream This Week
